config.seed_text: The last librarian trained a language model on a dead civilization's poetry and discovered that its footnotes were predictions.

model.notes: Direct continuation of the current repo's GPT-2 lineage.

records.reflection_text: The final LISP class will be teaching this subject at Stanford University, as well! Please note I am not affiliated with these labs (which would make it impossible to teach students), but my funding is for textbooks created by undergraduates who have already completed their masters or PhD programs there; they may also become interested into using them elsewhere during time spent abroad?
records.source_window_text: The last librarian trained a language model on a dead civilization's poetry and discovered that its footnotes were predictions.
records.reflection_window_text: The final LISP class will be teaching this subject at Stanford University, as well! Please note I am not affiliated with these labs (which would make it impossible to teach students), but my funding is for textbooks created by undergraduates who have already completed their masters or PhD programs there; they may also become interested into using them elsewhere during time spent abroad?

reflection_generation.prompt:
You are a text-only language model participating in a controlled recursion experiment.

Analyze the following text and do two things:
1. State its strongest recurring ideas in 2-4 short bullet points.
2. Produce a revised continuation that makes those ideas more structurally coherent and more surprising.

TEXT:
The last librarian trained a language model on a dead civilization's poetry and discovered that its footnotes were predictions.
reflection_generation.full_text:
You are a text-only language model participating in a controlled recursion experiment.

Analyze the following text and do two things:
1. State its strongest recurring ideas in 2-4 short bullet points.
2. Produce a revised continuation that makes those ideas more structurally coherent and more surprising.

TEXT:
The last librarian trained a language model on a dead civilization's poetry and discovered that its footnotes were predictions. The final LISP class will be teaching this subject at Stanford University, as well! Please note I am not affiliated with these labs (which would make it impossible to teach students), but my funding is for textbooks created by undergraduates who have already completed their masters or PhD programs there; they may also become interested into using them elsewhere during time spent abroad?
reflection_generation.continuation_text: The final LISP class will be teaching this subject at Stanford University, as well! Please note I am not affiliated with these labs (which would make it impossible to teach students), but my funding is for textbooks created by undergraduates who have already completed their masters or PhD programs there; they may also become interested into using them elsewhere during time spent abroad?

Use the reflection below to write the next evolved version of the text. Keep it as connected prose, not bullet points.

REFLECTION:

Please note I am not affiliated with these labs (which would make it impossible to teach students), but my funding is for textbooks created by undergraduates who have already completed their masters or PhD programs there; they may also become interested into using them elsewhere during time spent abroad?

REFLECTION:

Please note I am not affiliated with these labs (which would make it impossible to teach students), but my funding is for textbooks created by undergraduates who have already completed their masters or PhD programs there; they may also become interested into using them elsewhere during time spent abroad? Thank you so much!!
